title: Porter’s Five Forces

content.statement: Industry profitability is shaped by rivalry, new entrants, buyer power, supplier power, and substitutes.
content.question: Which of the five forces is currently compressing your margins most, and what move would weaken it?
content.equation: Profit Pool = 50 − (Rivalry + Entrants + Buyer + Supplier + Substitutes)
content.mental_model: Pentagon, five forces shaping how much profit any company can keep.
content.business_lens: Assess each force; design strategy to weaken adverse forces (e.g., switching costs to reduce buyer power).
content.life_lens: In any market you enter (career, business), map the forces before betting on a position.
sop: Score each of the five forces high/medium/low (see calculation, page 6)
Identify which forces compress profit most
Avoid industries where most forces are unfavorable
content.explainer: Telecom carriers face high rivalry and substitutes; they win by raising switching costs (contracts, bundled services). Retail faces high buyer power; private label and loyalty programs are responses. The framework is durable because the forces themselves are durable.
questions: Which of the five forces is currently compressing your margins most, and what move would weaken it?
If a new competitor entered tomorrow with venture funding, which force would hurt you first?
What's stopping your customers from switching to a competitor today besides your own goodwill?

definitions.def: A framework for analyzing the five competitive pressures, rivalry, new entrants, buyer power, supplier power, and substitutes, that determine how much profit an industry can retain.

content.equation_example: Score each force 1-10 for how much it compresses your margins: Rivalry 7, New Entrants 4, Buyer Power 8, Supplier Power 3, Substitutes 5. Total pressure = 7 + 4 + 8 + 3 + 5 = 27 out of a possible 50, so Profit Pool = 50 − 27 = 23. A higher pressure total means less of the industry's profit pool is available to you; a lower total means the industry structure itself is working in your favor.
content.closing_statement: Structure decides how much profit you get to keep.

caption:
Principle Nº 285: Porter’s Five Forces

Industry profitability is shaped by rivalry, new entrants, buyer power, supplier power, and substitutes.

Ask yourself: Which of the five forces is currently compressing your margins most, and what move would weaken it?
